faqs.question: Does my restaurant need a grease trap?
faqs.answer: Yes, {city} County requires all food service establishments to have a properly sized and maintained grease trap or interceptor to prevent fats, oils, and grease from entering the sewer system.

faqs.question: What size grease trap do I need for my business?
faqs.answer: The required size depends on your kitchen's flow rate, number of fixtures, and local code requirements. We assess your facility and install the right size to keep you compliant and clog-free.

faqs.question: How often should a grease trap be cleaned?
faqs.answer: Most commercial grease traps should be cleaned every one to three months depending on usage volume. Regular cleaning prevents backups and keeps you in compliance. Call {phone} to schedule service.
